title: Club Overview
content: Sporting Lagos Football Club (abbreviated SLFC) is a professional football club based in Lagos, Nigeria, competing in the Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL). The club was founded on February 3, 2022, by Shola Akinlade, the co-founder of Paystack, one of Africa's most successful fintech companies. Sporting Lagos is nicknamed 'The Noisy Lagosians' and 'The Tech Boys', reflecting both the passionate Lagos fanbase and the club's connection to Nigeria's technology ecosystem. The club's chairman is Godwin Enakhena.

title: Club Colors and Identity
content: Sporting Lagos FC plays in neon blue and Pantone blue for home fixtures, and yellow for away fixtures. The club's official colors are neon blue (#00BFFF), Pantone blue (#003087), and yellow (#FFD700). The nicknames 'The Noisy Lagosians' and 'The Tech Boys' represent the club's dual identity — rooted in Lagos street culture and connected to Nigeria's booming technology scene. The club's abbreviation is SLFC.

title: Stadium — Mobolaji Johnson Arena
content: Sporting Lagos FC plays their home matches at Mobolaji Johnson Arena, also known as Onikan Stadium, located in the Onikan district of Lagos Island. The stadium has a capacity of 10,000 spectators. Mobolaji Johnson Arena is one of Lagos's historic football venues, situated close to the Lagos waterfront. The stadium is named after Mobolaji Johnson, a former military governor of Lagos State. The Onikan area is central to Lagos Island, making it easily accessible for fans across the metropolitan area.

title: Ownership and Founding Story
content: Sporting Lagos FC was founded on February 3, 2022, by Shola Akinlade. Akinlade is the co-founder and CEO of Paystack, the Nigerian payment processing company that was acquired by Stripe in 2020 in one of the largest technology acquisitions in African history. Akinlade's vision for Sporting Lagos is to build a modern, professionally run football club that represents Lagos on the biggest stages in African football. The club is seen as part of a new wave of tech-entrepreneur-backed football clubs in Africa, bringing modern management practices, data analytics, and a fan-first approach to Nigerian football.

title: Head Coach — Jeffrey Buter
content: Jeffrey Buter is the Head Coach of Sporting Lagos FC. He leads the first team's tactical preparation and matchday operations. Under his guidance, the club has developed a structured playing style aimed at combining technical quality with the physicality required in the Nigeria Premier Football League.

title: Club Captain — Abdulraheem Suleiman
content: Abdulraheem Suleiman is the captain of Sporting Lagos FC. He wears the number 8 shirt and plays as a midfielder. As captain, Suleiman is the on-field leader of the team, marshalling the midfield and setting the tempo for the club's play. His leadership and experience are central to Sporting Lagos's ambitions in the NPFL.

title: League and Competition
content: Sporting Lagos FC competes in the Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL), which is the top tier of professional football in Nigeria. The NPFL is organized by the League Management Company (LMC) and features clubs from across Nigeria competing for the national championship. Sporting Lagos represents Lagos State in the league, one of the most competitive footballing regions in the country. The club aspires to qualify for continental competitions such as the CAF Champions League and the CAF Confederation Cup.

title: Rivals — Lagos Derby
content: Sporting Lagos FC's biggest rivals are Remo Stars FC and Ikorodu City FC. Matches against these clubs are considered Lagos Derby fixtures and carry enormous significance for fans and the city. Remo Stars FC, based in Ikenne-Remo in Ogun State (in the greater Lagos catchment area), are one of the NPFL's best-run clubs and represent the most high-profile rivalry for Sporting Lagos. Ikorodu City FC, based in Ikorodu in Lagos State, are the other key Lagos rival. Derby matches are the most anticipated fixtures on the Sporting Lagos calendar.

title: Academy — Gothia Cup Victory
content: Sporting Lagos FC has a youth academy that develops young Nigerian football talent. In a landmark achievement for the club, the Sporting Lagos academy team won the Gothia Cup Under-17 tournament in Sweden in 2024. The Gothia Cup is one of the world's largest and most prestigious youth football tournaments, held annually in Gothenburg, Sweden. Winning it announced Sporting Lagos to an international audience and demonstrated the quality of the club's youth development program. The victory was celebrated as a historic moment for Lagos football.

title: Ebenezer Harcourt — Historic Achievement
content: Ebenezer Harcourt, associated with Sporting Lagos FC, made history in 2025 by becoming Nigeria's youngest senior international, earning a call-up to the Super Eagles national team. This achievement represents a significant milestone not just for the player but for the club's player development and scouting program. Harcourt's rise through the ranks to represent Nigeria at the senior level is a testament to the quality of development at Sporting Lagos.

title: Fan Culture and Community
content: Sporting Lagos FC has quickly built a passionate and vocal fanbase since its founding in 2022. The nickname 'The Noisy Lagosians' reflects the energy and volume of the club's supporters. Lagos is one of Africa's largest cities with a massive football-loving population, and Sporting Lagos has connected with young, tech-savvy Lagosians who see the club as representing a new vision for Nigerian football. The club engages fans through social media, community events, and a modern approach to supporter relations. Matchdays at Mobolaji Johnson Arena are vibrant occasions driven by the energy of the home faithful.

title: Club Mission and Vision
content: Sporting Lagos FC's mission is to build a world-class football club rooted in Lagos that can compete at the highest levels of African football. Founded by a technology entrepreneur, the club embraces innovation, data-driven decision making, and modern club management practices. The vision is for Sporting Lagos to become a benchmark for how African football clubs can be run professionally, developing local talent, connecting with the community, and representing Nigeria on the continental and global stage. The club is nicknamed 'The Tech Boys' in part because of this forward-thinking, innovation-first philosophy.

title: SONA — Club AI Assistant
content: SONA is the official AI assistant of Sporting Lagos FC, built to serve fans with information about the club. SONA can answer questions about match fixtures, club history, player information, ticket details, and general club news. SONA is powered by the NEXUS, an AI operating system developed specifically for Sporting Lagos. The name SONA reflects the club's identity as 'The Tech Boys' — a tech-first football club that uses artificial intelligence to enhance the fan experience. Fans can chat with SONA on the club website or via WhatsApp.

title: Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL)
content: The Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) is the top professional football league in Nigeria. It consists of 20 clubs from across the country competing in a home-and-away format over a full season. The NPFL champion earns a place in the CAF Champions League, while other top finishers qualify for the CAF Confederation Cup. The league is known for its passionate atmosphere, physical style of play, and the quality of Nigerian talent it develops for clubs around the world. Sporting Lagos competes in the NPFL representing Lagos, the country's commercial capital and its most populous city.

title: Paystack and the Tech Connection
content: Sporting Lagos FC was founded by Shola Akinlade, who co-founded Paystack in 2015 alongside Ezra Olubi. Paystack became Nigeria's leading payment processing platform, processing billions of naira in transactions for businesses across Africa. In 2020, Stripe acquired Paystack for a reported $200 million, making it one of the largest tech acquisitions in African history. Akinlade used the success of Paystack to pursue his passion for football, founding Sporting Lagos in 2022. The club's 'Tech Boys' nickname is a nod to this background, and the club brings Silicon Valley-style thinking to Nigerian football — data analytics, professional management, and a focus on long-term club building.
